description: Immutable record of every create, update, and delete operation performed on any entity across all modules. Written by MDM infrastructure routines — never directly by module code. Provides full traceability of who changed what and when.
consumers: any module that calls AuditLog.record() — read access via AuditLog.findByEntity() and AuditLog.findByActor()

Id.constraints: primary key, generated by MDM routine, never by caller

EntityId.constraints: id of the affected entity in its own module table

Action.constraints: 'restore' used when a soft-deleted record is reactivated

ActorId.constraints: userId, agentId, or system service identifier

ActorType.constraints: 'system' for scheduled jobs and automated routines

Routine.constraints: camelCase routine name that triggered the change, e.g. 'purchaseOrderApprove'

Diff.constraints: direct output of microdiff(before, after) — array of change objects, each with: type ('CHANGE' | 'CREATE' | 'REMOVE'), path (array of keys, supports nested Details), oldValue (previous value), value (new value). Null on Action='create' (no before exists). Null on Action='delete' (full snapshot lives in the entity's own DynamoDB record). On Action='update' always required — caller must provide before snapshot. Supports deep diff inside Details JSONB field via path array.

CreatedAt.constraints: set by MDM routine at write time, never by caller

postgresql.note: primary query — history of a specific entity
what did this user or agent do
retention job — delete records older than retention window

dynamodb.note: enables time-ordered range query per entity — Diff stored here only, keeping PostgreSQL index lean

consistencyContract.note: audit log has no value if it arrives late — both stores are written in the same request before returning to caller. Diff is written to DynamoDB only — PostgreSQL index stays lean without the payload

retention.postgresql: configurable per installation, default 90 days — enforced by weekly cleanup job
retention.dynamodb: permanent until explicit deletion — full diff history preserved

persistence.note: records are never updated or deleted by application code — only the retention job may delete from PostgreSQL
